stepAnswer: The sample is representative of our population
stepTitle: C) When we conduct a bootstrap resample, what is the underlying $$\frac{assumption}{reasoning}$$ for resampling from our sample? Why does it work?
choices: The sample is representative of our population
Resampling eliminates bias in the original estimator
The population distribution is known, allowing resampling to accurately reflect true variability
Resampling from our sample guarantees each bootstrap sample is independent from the original
